cards.back: A disturbance that transfers energy through matter or space without transferring matter.
A wave that requires a medium (e.g., sound, water waves).
A wave that does not require a medium (e.g., light, radio waves).
Wave where particle motion is perpendicular to wave direction (e.g., light, waves on a string).
Wave where particle motion is parallel to wave direction (e.g., sound).
Distance between two consecutive identical points on a wave.
Number of complete oscillations per second (units: Hz).
Time for one complete oscillation: $T = \frac{1}{f}$
Maximum displacement from equilibrium position.
$v = f\lambda = \frac{\lambda}{T}$

cards.front: What is the **speed of sound** in air at room temperature?
cards.back: Approximately $343 \text{ m/s}$ (at $20^\circ\text{C}$).

cards.front: How does **temperature** affect speed of sound?
cards.back: Speed increases with increasing temperature: $v \propto \sqrt{T}$
When two or more waves overlap, combining to form a resultant wave.
Waves in phase combine to produce larger amplitude.
Waves out of phase combine to produce smaller amplitude.
The net displacement at any point is the sum of individual wave displacements.
Wave pattern from interference of two identical waves traveling in opposite directions.
Point on a standing wave with zero amplitude (no motion).
Point on a standing wave with maximum amplitude.
Large amplitude oscillation when driving frequency matches natural frequency.
Lowest resonant frequency of a system.
Integer multiples of the fundamental frequency.

cards.front: What is the **speed of wave on a string**?
cards.back: $v = \sqrt{\frac{F_T}{\mu}}$, where $F_T$ is tension and $\mu$ is linear mass density.
Mass per unit length: $\mu = \frac{m}{L}$
Change in observed frequency due to relative motion of source and observer.

cards.front: What is the **Doppler formula** for sound?
cards.back: $f' = f\left(\frac{v \pm v_o}{v \mp v_s}\right)$

cards.front: What happens to frequency when source moves **toward** observer?
cards.back: Observed frequency increases (higher pitch).

cards.front: What happens to frequency when source moves **away** from observer?
cards.back: Observed frequency decreases (lower pitch).
Wave formed when source moves faster than wave speed.
Shock wave from object exceeding speed of sound.
Ratio of object speed to speed of sound: $M = \frac{v}{v_s}$
Power per unit area: $I = \frac{P}{A}$ (units: $\text{W/m}^2$).

cards.front: What is the **inverse square law** for intensity?
cards.back: $I \propto \frac{1}{r^2}$
$\beta = 10\log\left(\frac{I}{I_0}\right)$ dB, where $I_0 = 10^{-12} \text{ W/m}^2$

cards.front: What unit is used for **sound intensity level**?
cards.back: Decibel (dB).
Amplitude (intensity) of sound wave.
Frequency of sound wave.
Quality of sound determined by harmonic content.
Periodic variation in amplitude from interference of two slightly different frequencies.
